zh.Nether Whitacre, Warwickshire, England: 英國瓦維其可爾瑟 塔可
zh.Tamworth, Warwickshire, England: 英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Robert de Marmion於1263年出生在英國瓦維其可爾瑟 塔可時，他的父親Philip Marmion54歲，母親Joan Kilpec38歲。他於大約1299年與Isabella Fitz Ralph在英國英格蘭素瑞結婚。他們至少是4個女兒的父母。他去世於1316年在英國斯塔福爾塔沃斯享年53歲。

zh-hans.Nether Whitacre, Warwickshire, England: 英国瓦維其可爾瑟 塔可
zh-hans.Tamworth, Warwickshire, England: 英国英格兰瓦維其可爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Robert de Marmion于1263年出生在英国瓦維其可爾瑟 塔可时，他的父亲Philip Marmion54岁，母亲Joan Kilpec38岁。他于大约1299年与Isabella Fitz Ralph在英国英格兰素瑞结婚。他们至少是4个女儿的父母。他去世于1316年在英国斯塔福爾塔沃斯享年53岁。

nameMeanings.name: Marmion
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish: perhaps an Anglicized form of an unidentified Gaelic name. Compare   Merriman  .
English (Lancashire; of Norman origin) and French: nickname from Old French marmion ‘monkey, brat’.

variants.name: Merriman
meanings: nickname from Middle English mirie, merie, murie ‘merry, pleasant’ + man ‘man’. Compare Merry 1. This surname is established in Ireland (perhaps as an Anglicized form of an unidentified Gaelic name; compare Marmion ) as well as England.

there has probably been some confusion with Marriman, probably an occupational name meaning ‘servant of Mary’, from the female personal name Mary (see Marie ) + Middle English man in the sense ‘servant’, or perhaps a pet form of Mary using the hypocoristic suffix -man. The surname Marriman is now vanishingly rare in Britain and Ireland.

variants.name: Marmon
meanings: variant of Marriman, an occupational name meaning ‘servant of Mary’, from the female personal name Mary (see Marie ) + Middle English man in the sense ‘servant’.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Robert de Marmion was born in 1263, in Nether Whitacre, Warwickshire, England, his father, Philip Marmion, was 54 and his mother, Joan Kilpec, was 38. He married Isabella Fitz Ralph about 1299, in Surrey, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 daughters. He died in 1316, in Tamworth, Staffordshire, England, at the age of 53.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Robert de Marmion est né en 1263, à Nether Whitacre, Warwickshire, Angleterre, son père, Philip Marmion, avait 54 ans et sa mère, Joan Kilpec, avait 38 ans. Il a épousé Isabella Fitz Ralph environ 1299, à Surrey, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 filles. Il est décédé en 1316, à Tamworth, Staffordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 53 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Robert de Marmion 1263 in Nether Whitacre, Warwickshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Philip Marmion 54 und seine Mutter Joan Kilpec 38. Er hat um 1299 in Surrey, England, United Kingdom Isabella Fitz Ralph geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Töchter. Er starb 1316 im Alter von 53 Jahren in Tamworth, Staffordshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Robert de Marmion è nato il nel 1263 nel Nether Whitacre, Warwickshire, England, il suo padre, Philip Marmion, aveva 54 anni e il suo madre, Joan Kilpec, aveva 38 anni. Lui sposato Isabella Fitz Ralph circa 1299, nel Surrey, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figlie. Morì nel 1316, nel Tamworth, Staffordshire, England, all'età di 53 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Robert de Marmionが1263年にイギリスワーウィクシュアイアネスエル ウィタクアで生まれた時，父Philip Marmionは54歳，母Joan Kilpecは38歳であった。約1299年にイギリスイギリススリーでIsabella Fitz Ralphと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の娘の親であった。彼は1316年にイギリススタフォードシュアイアタムウオルスで53歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Robert de Marmion(이)가 1263년 영국 워윅실르 네터 휘타크르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Philip Marmion(은)는 54세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan Kilpec(은)는 38세였다. 그는 대략 1299년 영국 잉글랜드 수리에서 Isabella Fitz Ralph(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1316년 영국 스태퍼드실르 타워스에서 53세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Robert de Marmion nasceu em 1263, em Nether Whitacre, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Philip Marmion, tinha 54 anos e sua mãe, Joan Kilpec, tinha 38 anos. Ele casou-se com Isabella Fitz Ralph aproximadamente 1299, em Surrey, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1316, em Tamworth, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, com 53 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Robert de Marmion родился в 1263 г. в местности нетир уитакр, варвиккшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Philip Marmion было 54 года, а матери, которую звали Joan Kilpec, 38. Он женился на женщине по имени Isabella Fitz Ralph около 1299 г. в местности сурри, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 дочери. Он умер в 1316 г. в местности тамворт, стаффордшир, Англия в возрасте 53 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Robert de Marmion nació en 1263, en Nether Whitacre, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Philip Marmion, tenía 54 años y su mamá, Joan Kilpec, tenía 38 años. Se casó con Isabella Fitz Ralph aproximadamente 1299, en Surrey, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijas. Murió en 1316, en Tamworth, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 53 años.
